[1] Hi Jane,
I’ll be home late tonight. Can you cook? Or I can get something on the way home. Fish and chips?
Michael

[2] High-speed internet offer:
Buy for 12 months and pay half-price!
Offer ends soon.

[3] Mark,
Sorry I missed you. I had to go out to my mother's house. Can we do it tomorrow? Same time and place?
Hannah

[4] STAFF PARKING ONLY
For extra spaces and student parking, turn left

[5] Hello,
Is your bike still for sale? I'm interested in it if it doesn't need any work and I can test ride it.
Regards,
Ben

[6] To
:         Joseph
From
:    Briony
Dear Joseph,
I'm unhappy that you were late a second time this week. Please make an appointment with Jenny to speak to me.
Regards,
Briony

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt:
Hi Jane,
I’ll be home late tonight. Can you cook? Or I can get something on the way home. Fish and chips?
Michael

options.label: Michael wants Jane to cook fish and chips.
Michael wants to eat fish and chips on the way home.
Michael offers to buy fish and chips for dinner.

questions.prompt:
High-speed internet offer:
Buy for 12 months and pay half-price!
Offer ends soon.

options.label: You get a special price on six months of internet.
You get a better price if you buy internet for a year.
You don't have to decide quickly.

questions.prompt:
Mark,
Sorry I missed you. I had to go out to my mother's house. Can we do it tomorrow? Same time and place?
Hannah

options.label: Hannah wants to meet Mark at her mother's house tomorrow.
Hannah misses Mark and wants to go to his house.
Mark went to Hannah's house but she was not at home.

questions.prompt:
STAFF PARKING ONLY
For extra spaces and student parking, turn left

options.label: This is the only place where staff can park.
Extra space is only for students.
Turn left if you are staff and the car park is full.

questions.prompt:
Hello,
Is your bike still for sale? I'm interested in it if it doesn't need any work and I can test ride it.
Regards,
Ben

options.label: Ben wants to see if he likes the bike.
Ben doesn’t need the bike for work.

questions.prompt:
To
:         Joseph
From
:    Briony
Dear Joseph,
I'm unhappy that you were late a second time this week. Please make an appointment with Jenny to speak to me.
Regards,
Briony

options.label: Briony will be unhappy if Joseph is late for a second time this week.
Briony wants Joseph to explain to her why he was late.
Joseph must explain why he was late to Jenny.

passage.label: Gerald
passage.text: When I was a boy, my parents worked in Africa for two years. We visited lots of exciting places there and I have loved travelling and exploring since then. I usually choose to travel alone because it is easier to meet people that way. I often stay with the new friends I make on my journeys, and finding out about new cultures is my favourite part of exploring.

passage.label: Simone
passage.text: My favourite places are in the cold parts of the world. There are some areas, covered in snow and ice, where almost no one has ever been before. I usually go on foot because there are no roads. I plan my trips very carefully - I like to be well prepared and I take lots of special equipment with me. I sometimes go for days and weeks without seeing other people - I like the opportunity that gives me to think about my life and to make plans.

passage.label: Frank
passage.text: I do most of my exploring by boat. It can take me weeks to get somewhere I could drive to in a day, but it is all about the journey. I always take someone else because my boat needs two people and I don’t think I would like to go alone. There usually aren’t many people at all in the places that I explore, so I don’t often meet new people. My boat is quite small so I can get to places that other, bigger boats can’t get to. That makes travelling even more interesting.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.prompt: Who likes learning about the different ways that people live?
Who explores places that you cannot get to by car?
Who has to travel with another person?
Who thinks a lot about what to take with them?
Who spent time away from home when they were young?
Who explains why their transport is better than others?

passage.text:
Henrietta Dawson, a businessperson from Portsmouth, is the new Businessperson of the Year. Henrietta heard she had won the prize at a dinner in London last night.
‘I can't believe I won the title!’ Henrietta said when she found out. ‘There are hundreds of good, hard-working businesspeople out there, so when people tell you that you're the best it's just fantastic.’
Henrietta runs three companies that make food and drink to sell to hotels and restaurants in and around her hometown. ‘I couldn’t have done it without my parents and husband. They've given me a lot of help,’ Henrietta said.
Henrietta's mother was also a successful businessperson and taught her daughter that anything is possible if you work hard. Henrietta is also lucky because her husband, Pete, put his own career on hold to look after their two young children.
Henrietta is the first woman to win the title. Last year’s winner, Tom Lawson, who runs a successful tourism company, was at the dinner too. ‘The competition is an important way to get people interested in starting their own businesses,’ he told the audience. ‘Of course, not everyone wants to do it, but you don't need to come from a rich family to be a successful businessperson.’
Henrietta could one day be a rich woman. But she isn’t one yet; the money she makes goes back into her businesses, not into her pocket.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

options.label: has food and drink companies in Portsmouth.
sells hotels and restaurants in her hometown.
has companies which sell food and drink in London.
wasn't surprised when she won the title.
was told by many businesspeople that she was the best.
didn't know she would win the competition.

questions.prompt: Tom thinks the competition is important

options.label: because everyone is interested in starting a business.
because it shows that business is not only for some people.
because anyone can run a business.
might be rich in the future.
uses her money to start new businesses.

passage.text:
Florence Nightingale was born in 1820. Her parents gave her an excellent education, but two hundred years ago the daughters of rich parents didn’t have careers. Their families expected them to (19) ............ married and stay at home. But Florence wanted to be a nurse and (20) ............ sick people. In 1854 she travelled to Turkey. At that time, Britain and some other countries were fighting a war against Russia. Florence worked very (21) ............ to improve the care of injured soldiers in the hospital. She became (22) ............ all over the world for her work there.
Florence started a school for nurses in 1864 and, for the first (23) ............., being a nurse became a real job. She also understood that it was very important to (24) ............ things clean in hospitals. Florence died in 1910 at the age of ninety.

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, choose the correct answer.

passage.text:
From:
John
To:
Mark
Dear Mark,
I hope (0) ............ and your family are well. I am visiting London next week and I thought it would be nice (25) ............ meet. We haven't seen each other (26) ............ a long time! I am staying in London (27) ............ Tuesday to Sunday evening. (28) ............ you like to meet me somewhere for a snack or (29) ............ meal? I am busy most days with meetings, but I'll be free (30) ............ the evenings and at the weekend. It would be great to see you again.
Best wishes,
John

questionGroups.instruction: For each question, write the correct answer. Write ONE word for each gap.

passage.text:
You want your English friend, Roger, to buy you some fruit and vegetables from the supermarket.
Write a note to Roger.
In your note:
•   ask Roger to buy you some fruit and vegetables from the supermarket
•   say what fruit and vegetables you want
•   say when you will give him the money.

questions.prompt: Write your email in the box on the right.
Look at the three pictures. Write the story shown in the pictures. Write 35 words or more.
